hypothesis: If open-weight language models initially SFT on alloy composition triplets are further optimized with direct preference optimization using Thermo-Calc–derived hierarchical rewards, then they will generate BCC/B2 superalloy composition pairs that more frequently satisfy the target phase objectives and show higher validity, coverage, and novelty than SFT-only and prompt-based baselines because physics-based preference feedback aligns the model’s generative distribution with thermodynamic constraints while limiting distribution shift.
